title: Bone, dentin and cementum differentially influence the differentiation of osteoclast-like cells
author: Both, Annika
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: We investigated how different oral hard tissues determine the differentiation of osteoclast-like cells. Murine macrophage cells were stimulated for 12 d with RANKL and M-CSF on pulverized bone, dentin, cementum or polystyrene – with and without stimulation. To elucidate total gene expression, RNA sequencing was carried out.
steps_raw: We investigated how different oral hard tissues determine the differentiation of osteoclast-like cells. Murine macrophage cells were stimulated for 12 d with RANKL and M-CSF on pulverized bone, dentin, cementum or polystyrene – with and without stimulation. To elucidate total gene expression, RNA sequencing was carried out.
